Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(22 comments)

This breaks the support of several changelog readers.

lfs changelog should return all records that at least one reader has not cleared (here: truncation happens on first clear).

unregistering a reader should clear the records up to the minimum one that at least one reader has not cleared (here: truncate everything)

Patch Set 2:

Henri, part of what I'm trying to fix is that there is no response to the client when things fail.

A simple example:  have 2 or 3 CL users.  Try and delete one that does not exist (say cl45).  Output looks like this:

[root@centss05 lustre-release]# lctl --device lustre-MDT0000 changelog_deregister cl45
[root@centss05 lustre-release]# echo $?
0
[root@centss05 lustre-release]#

There is *no* indication to the user that anything failed.  In fact, it looks exactly like success.  The same happens for records that are out of range as well.

Changing the behavior of my patch so that it clears out the correct items for a changelog_clear, is obviously critically important, and I'll work on that.

[root@centss05 lustre-release]# lctl --device lustre-MDT0000 changelog_deregister cl45 [root@centss05 lustre-release]# echo $? 0 [root@centss05 lustre-release]#

There is *no* indication to the user that anything failed. In fact, it looks exactly like success. The same happens for records that are out of range as well.

Errors _are_ properly propagated. The problems you observed are due to the fact that no errors are returned in the first place in these cases.

  - "found" flag not checked when deregistering a non-existing user
  - an arguable feature that transparently change an invalid record number to clear into current max.

Henri: I'd be happy to change the commit message, how would you like it to read?

For my part for the user of liblustreapi, the return codes did not distinguish between error and success at all.   The return code from the IOCTL was effectively meaningless.  Printing errors into dmesg on the mgs was the only sort of failure message that could be received, and at least for the users of lfs, they may not even have access to that.

As reported in an earlier version, this patch breaks multi-consumers support. Say you have cl1 & cl2 registered, unregistering cl2 should not clear all records!

Try it by yourself:
- register cl1
- touch /mnt/lustre/file.{0..9}
- register cl2
- lfs changelog displays all records, OK
- deregister cl2
- lfs changelog no longer shows records!

Actually, this change could even add a test to prove itself sane.

Also:

I cannot reproduce the error that you are attempting to fix: on master (4af430) unregistering a changelog user that does not exist fails, displays an error and returns ENOENT. Idem for invalid user on changelog_clear.


[root@ludev]# lctl --device lustre-MDT0000 changelog_deregister cl36
error: changelog_deregister: No such file or directory
[root@ludev]# echo $?
2

The second "issue" (silent success when clearing an old / future entry) is acceptable, IMHO.

This said, I like the code split :-)

Patch Set 15: Code-Review-1

(12 comments)

There is still a bug when some client tries to call "changelog_clear 0".

To reproduce:

    - register cl1
    - register cl2
    - touch /mnt/lustre/file.{0..9}
    - lfs changelog_clear cl2 0
    - lfs changelog # Shows nothing, all records were dropped

I explain in the comment where this is coming from.
To fix it I suggest the behaviour of mdd_changelog_llog_cancel should be modified so that 0 does not mean "drop everything" anymore (this is counter-intuitive and ULLONG_MAX does the job just as well) but means "do nothing". This would allow to remove all the conditions like "mcu[cp]_minrec != 0" that are currently used. The faulty behaviour also disappears thanks to that.

commit.subject: LU-7670 mdt: allow changelog commands to return errors
commit.message:
LU-7670 mdt: allow changelog commands to return errors

Return errors to lctl/lfs users for out of range, and invalid IDs.
Currently only 0 is ever returned, regardless of outcome.  Some
information is printed in the MDS logs, but nothing on the client
to indicate success or failure.

Split purge and clear code into 2 separate paths.
